'bar' ) ; } [$[Get Code]] Then if an accessor specifies the following: exports. setup = function ( ) { implement ( 'MyInterface' ) ; } [$[Get Code]] then it will also have a parameter named 'foo' and an output named 'bar'. A derived accessor may mod

an output named 'bar'. A derived accessor may modify the options of the interface specification. For example, exports. setup = function ( ) { implement ( 'MyInterface' ) ; parameter ( 'foo' , { 'value' : 43 } ) ; } [$[Get Code]] modifies the default

e' : 43 } ) ; } [$[Get Code]] modifies the default value of 'foo' to 43. See Lee and Seshia, Chapter 14 for the meaning interface inheritance. See Support for implement and extend in Ptolemy II . Back to accessor specification Edit - History - Print
